characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A Secret Service agent, former Marine, and recovering alcoholic. Lattimer gets 'vibes' that tells him when something strange or bad is about to happen; one of these vibes warned him of his father's death when he was a child, something he has grappled wtih ever since. He's reassigned to Warehouse 13 after witnessing Artie neutralize the Aztec Bloodstone, which nobody believes actually happened. While seemingly less mature than his partner, he is still a highly competent agent. Agent Mulder: To Myka's Agent Scully, though he's far more relaxed than the namesake. It has been said that he is the intuitive side of his partnership with Myka.Reversed in "Past Imperfect," where Myka is the emotional, impulsive one while Pete is the methodical, logical one. Justified, as in this case Myka is investigating something personal, and Pete realizes he needs to pick up the slack.Myka:Pete, what are you doing?Pete:Well, I'm thinking like Myka. When something doesn't make sense, Myka tries to make sense of it. But not right now, because she's too emotional, which is where Pete usually is, emotional. Then I thought hey, while you're being me, I may as well be you. Reversed in "Past Imperfect," where Myka is the emotional, impulsive one while Pete is the methodical, logical one. Justified, as in this case Myka is investigating something personal, and Pete realizes he needs to pick up the slack.Myka:Pete, what are you doing?Pete:Well, I'm thinking like Myka. When something doesn't make sense, Myka tries to make sense of it. But not right now, because she's too emotional, which is where Pete usually is, emotional. Then I thought hey, while you're being me, I may as well be you. The Alcoholic: Pete is a teetotal alcoholic, having been sober for several years at the beginning of the series. Tellingly, he shows clear signs of panic in "Merge With Caution" when Myka and he switch bodies when Myka was inebriated, fearing that this will cause him to suffer a relapse. The Atoner: For his relati

characters.name: Myka Bering
characters.description: A Secret Service agent with a no-nonsense attitude and a healty dose of skepticism. Her partner (both in the romantic and professional senses), Sam Martino, was killed while protecting the President before the series began, and Myka has been grieving him since. She's assigned to Warehouse 13 along with Pete; her father owned a bookstore, and her knowledge of classic literature, history and mythology has helped save her life, and countless others, on countless occasions. Action Girl: She can take on four armed Marines while unarmed herself. Agent Scully: To Pete'sAgent Mulder. She has a knack for detail and has been described as meticulous to Pete's intuitiveness.Artie:This warehouse, well look, the warehouse needs you. Pete, don't touch! It needs your combined talents. He's intuitive and you've got a scrupulous eye for detail. He's scattershot, y'see, and you're meticulous. You look and he leaps. Arbitrary Skepticism: She's usually the first to point out how impossible whatever weirdness they encounter is, at which point Pete reminds her what they both do for a living. Badass Bookworm: Highlighted in the season 3 premiere, where she saves at least four people using her knowledge of Shakespeare plays; three of which are rescued withinsecondsof each other. Note that she has to do the following to save the victims: look at the page, see the pose and the name of the play, recognize the character, then recite the character's last words, which the victim must say before the page burns out. And she does it every time in less than five seconds.

characters.name: Artie Nielsen
characters.description: Ancient Keeper: Though Artie isn't really all thatancient(despiteClaudia's incessant teasing). And his job involves keeping the artifacts in the Warehouse, not giving them away. Ambiguously Jewish: He isn't shown outright to be Jewish until the Christmas episode in the second season. Big Ol' Eyebrows: Artie is remembered by Jinks as "the guy with the eyebrows". They are promptly exaggerated in Fargo's VR game in "Don't Hate the Player".Pete: Artie's gonna be deeply pissed.In "Insatiable," when Artie can't get the boy to open up to him, Pete remarks that "maybe it's the eyebrows." In "Insatiable," when Artie can't get the boy to open up to him, Pete remarks that "maybe it's the eyebrows." The Chessmaster: Of a literal sort, as shown in "Elements". Artie has a chessboard he keeps outside his office and plays against himself for months between moves. Given how Claudia ignores his objections and makes a move half-way through the episode, and then at the end makes the first move of a re-set board, she's also on-board for having this be her role.

characters.name: Claudia Donovan
characters.description: Acting Your Intellectual Age: Lampshaded in a conversation with Artie regarding her plans after saving Joshua:Artie:Shouldn't you be in college or something? Don't you wanna be with other people your own age?Claudia:Artie,I'm not my own age.Come on; writing English papers and going to frat parties?Boring. And This Is for...: A bone-chilling version of it in the Season 3 finale.Claudia:That was forSteve. Badass Bookworm: Just as much as Myka. Broken Bird: At first. She reverts to this in the season 3 finale aftershe sees Steve's dead body, and eventually recovers after he's revived in "An Evil Within". Butt-Monkey: In season one, though doesn't completely leave this territory. Hell, Season 4 actually had several multi-episode plots that amounted to 'Claudia receives some form of physical pain', with varying levels of severity and comedy to the situation.

characters.name: Steve Jinks
characters.description: Back from the Dead: Claudiaresurrects him using the metronome. Bad Liar: In contrast to his ability to sense other people's lies, he's a terrible liar himself.Though considering howhe was able to trick Walter Sykes and everyone else into thinking he'd gone double-agent, one has to wonder if he's just bad at on-the-spot lying. Though considering howhe was able to trick Walter Sykes and everyone else into thinking he'd gone double-agent, one has to wonder if he's just bad at on-the-spot lying. Big Brother Instinct: In a big way towards Claudia.Steve:Because I couldn't savemy sister. But I can protect you. But Not Too Gay: Steve's sexuality is mostly just referred to, and he's never seen with a boyfriend due to beingCursed with Awesome. That and because the Warehouse is in the ass end of nowhere. Played with though in "Runaway"; while his relationship with his ex is built onNo Hugging, No Kissinguntil theyhave an offscreen sexual encounterat the end, they do spend the episode dealing with realisticUnresolved Sexual Tensionthe same way hetero couples in the show do and openly flirt when they finally talk about their issues (as well as prove to be a somewhat effectiveBattle Couple), all of which is still rare with gay couples in live action fiction.Subverted in "Savage Seduction," where Steve is split into two versions of himself: one aKnight Templarcop, the other an irresponsible and flirtatiousCamp Gay.

characters.name: JamesMacPherson
characters.description: Affably Evil: He remains ridiculously polite and well-mannered, even when placing people in mortal danger. Big Bad: Of season oneand "The Greatest Gift," the third season's Christmas episode. Big Bad Friend: He used to be Artie's partner when Artie was a field agent. The Chessmaster: Just as smart as Artie so he's always one step ahead. Disc-One Final Boss: To an extent,MacPhersonis this in Season 2. He was theBig Badof the first season and seemed set up to continue being so, only forH.G. Wells to kill himone episode into the season.

characters.name: H.G. Wells
characters.description: Affably Evil: So much so that, past her introduction episode in Season 2, you'd have a hard time believing she ever was evil until the finale.As of Season 3, she's quite firmly on the side of the angels. And I Must Scream: She was turned into a bronze statue, but aware for over100 years. She seems to have come out of it fairly alright, and just used it to plan. Anti-Villain: For most of her screentime.She eventually pulls aHeel–Face Turnand aHeroic Sacrifice. It's generally implied that HG isn't actually all that bad; it's just that losing her daughter didverybadthingsto her, and as a result, she has a tendency to temporarily revert toThe Dark Side. The Atoner: For Season Three. Badass Bookworm: A villainous version of Myka and Claudia.

characters.name: Walter Sykes
characters.description: Bad Boss: He's a big fan ofYou Have Outlived Your Usefulness. Big Bad: Of Season 3. The Corruption: The bracelet that gave him mobility also completely prevents its user from being able to give or receive love. Unfortunately, they failed to take it from him fast enough to prevent that effect from kicking in. Creepy Child: When he was younger, thanks toThe Corruption. Evil Cripple: He's a paraplegic, so he's in a wheelchair.

characters.name: Marcus Diamond
characters.description: The Dragon: To Sykes. Living on Borrowed Time:Which runs out when Claudia stops the metronome keeping his heart beating. Not Quite Dead: Pete throws him off a balcony and assumes he's dead, until he reappears beside Jinks, even wiping off some of his blood.It turns out Sykes had an artifact which was keeping him alive. Torture Technician: He claims to be one when interrogatingEmily Lake/H.G. Wells. We have no reason to disbelieve him.

characters.name: Sally Stukowski
characters.description: Torture Technician: She tortures at leastsevenregents to death.She is then tortured by Mrs. Frederic, though she escapes. You Have Outlived Your Usefulness:You'd think that she'd have seen it coming, seeing as she was the one who carried out Syke's elimination orders up to this point.

characters.name: Brother Adrian
characters.description: Anti-Villain: He does genuinely believe he's doing the right thing. Big Bad: For the first half of season 4. Church Militant: Works for the Black Diamond Brotherhood, a secret order of the Vatican dedicated to protecting Ferdinand Magellan's astrolabe. Enemy Within: The Brother Adrian of the new timeline never really existed. He's a manifestation of Artie's subconscious, the evil of the astrolabe given form. The most evil thing Artie could do would be to reverse the effect and create a world without hope, so that is what it drives him to do. Hypocrite: Gets on Artie's case for supposedly releasing some kind of evil, only to release highly dangerous artifacts which he stole from the Warehouse.In particular, he unleashes Alice Liddell, aBody Surfingpsychopath, to take out Artie's loved ones, with no apparent means of controlling her, care for what she'll do after that (not that it would matter; if Artie played ball, it would never have happened), or even any guarantee that she'll honor the agreement (which she ultimately did not).Averted as it turns out it was reallyArtie all along.

characters.name: Paracelsus
characters.description: Big Bad: His influence is felt throughout the second half of season 4, though he doesn't actually show up until the last few episodes. Cain and Abel: Has been waiting in bronze for alongtime to get revenge on his brotherSuttonfor turning him in to the authorities. Evil Is Hammy: Anthony Head isobviouslyenjoying himself. Evil Laugh: He has a magnificent one. Fallen Hero: He wasthe Caretaker of Warehouse 9.

characters.name: Mrs. Frederic
characters.description: Ancient Keeper: We initially think that the guardian of the Warehouse is Artie. We later find out it's her. Black Boss Lady: She's the Caretaker of the Warehouse and Artie's immediate superior. Came Back Wrong: Sort of. In the season 4 premiere, afterArtie hits theReset Buttonto save the Warehouse, she shows him her graying hair, suspecting him of having done something. Crazy-Prepared: She and the Regents seem to be prepared for just about any and all problems. Da Chief: She's Artie's boss.

characters.name: Leena
characters.description: '80s Hair: Naturally curly hair. Action Girl: Despite not being a field agent, she beats up a few bad guys by herself. Advertised Extra: Despite being listed in the starting credits as a regular, there were several episodes in season 2 where she wasn't seen. Aura Vision: Has the ability to see the auras of people, objects and places. Character Death: Courtesy ofEvil!Artie.

characters.name: Daniel Dickinson
characters.description: Back for the Dead: In season two. Dropped a Bridge on Him: He dies rather abruptly (and brutally!) about halfway through the second season, early in the episode "Vendetta", after having been absent since about halfway through the first season. Friend on the Force: He used to be Pete and Myka's boss before they joined the Warehouse and they called him a few times instead ofArtieto track down a lead.

characters.name: Benedict Valda
characters.description: Hero Antagonist: To a mild degree. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: He can be a massive hardass, but he ultimately only wants to protect the Warehouse, even being aReasonable Authority Figureand not holding Pete's actions while under the effects of an Artifact against him.He ultimately willingly performs aHeroic Sacrificeto allow the others to get inside Warehouse 2, saying he's just doing his job as a Regent.

characters.name: Jane Lattimer
characters.description: Action Mom: She's Pete's mother and she's picked up a few tricks through the years. Almighty Janitor: Pete's mother is an elementary school teacher.She claims that Regents are picked based on their areas of expertise; in her case, her insight on children. First-Name Basis: Despite being a Regent, even the agents only refer to her by her first name. Spider-Sense: Apparently, she passed her ability to have vibes on to her son. Troubled Backstory Flashback: She and Pete once used an artifact to look into her past to dig up information aboutWalter Sykes.

characters.name: Adwin Kosan
characters.description: Big Good: He's the leader of the Regents and the nominal head of Warehouse 13. Da Chief: He's introduced as the head of the Regents. Reasonable Authority Figure:When Pete inadvertently comes close to blowing the Warehouse's cover by arousing a doctor's suspicions, he gives Pete a chance to choose the best option of dealing with the problem, letting his vibes inform his judgment.When Janegoes rogue and allows Claudia to resurrect Jinks, he is stern but realistic in his response and ultimately allows the artifact to continue to be used to keep Steve alive and other than instituting supervisory precautions on the situation seemingly doesn't punish any of the Agents or Jane in any way. When Pete inadvertently comes close to blowing the Warehouse's cover by arousing a doctor's suspicions, he gives Pete a chance to choose the best option of dealing with the problem, letting his vibes inform his judgment. When Janegoes rogue and allows Claudia to resurrect Jinks, he is stern but realistic in his response and ultimately allows the artifact to continue to be used to keep Steve alive and other than instituting supervisory precautions on the situation seemingly doesn't punish any of the Agents or Jane in any way.

characters.name: Abigail Cho
characters.description: Naïve Newcomer: She's eager to help, but she doesn't know what she's getting into most of the time. Obfuscating Stupidity: Likely, given thatshe's the Warehouse's memory keeper. Secret-Keeper: She's the Warehouse'smemory safeguard. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: She was hired for this purpose, actually. Since Artie was being allHeroic BSoDover Leena's death Mrs Fredericks called her in to help him deal with it. She runs the inn and provides a sounding board for Artie when needed. The one thing Leena did that she can't is determine an artifact's origin. That and cook for the B&B, but according to Steve she orders in really well.

characters.name: Nick Powell
characters.description: Batman Gambit: Actually manages to pull off a pretty elaborate one againstJinks and Claudia in order to free Paracelsus. Human Resources: What he's being used for when the Warehouse agents save him. The Mole: For his mother, Charlotte. Street Urchin: Poses as one to get close to Claudia. Really Seven Hundred Years Old:Was made immortal with his parents, and hasn't aged since.
